name: Valley Pride
description_short: Valley Pride Federal Credit Union is a small healthcare-focused credit union in Kingston, PA, established in 1979, offering traditional banking services to over 600 members.
description_long: Valley Pride Federal Credit Union was established in 1979 and is headquartered in Kingston, Pennsylvania. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates as a member-owned financial cooperative designed to serve specific communities, particularly healthcare organizations and their employees. The institution has maintained its presence in northeastern Pennsylvania for over 45 years. Valley Pride offers a comprehensive range of consumer banking products including checking and savings accounts, credit cards, auto loans, personal loans, certificates of deposit (CDs), and IRA/KEOGH retirement accounts. Members also have access to e-statements, financial calculators, financial education resources, and online and mobile banking platforms. The credit union serves over 600 members and stewards approximately $6.6 million in assets through its single branch location at 480 Pierce Street in Kingston. The credit union caters specifically to employees and members of healthcare organizations, which distinguishes its membership eligibility criteria from traditional banks. With over 2 dedicated employees as of 2026, Valley Pride operates as a lean institution focused on personalized service. The organization maintains a community Q&A forum to help members with account and loan questions, and has received mixed member reviews ranging from praise for staff politeness and responsiveness to criticism regarding email communication responsiveness. As a small, locally-rooted credit union, Valley Pride appeals to members seeking community-based financial services and specialized healthcare sector focus. However, prospective members should note the institution's limited branch infrastructure (1 branch, 0 ATMs), modest asset size, and documented service gaps in digital communication channels. The current lobby closure and limited online functionality compared to larger institutions may present accessibility challenges for some consumers.
pros: Healthcare-focused membership eligibility targeting specific employment sectors
Established 45-year history of operation (founded 1979) providing institutional stability
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, loans, CDs, and retirement accounts
Member praise for staff politeness and responsiveness to customer needs
Online and mobile banking available for both iOS and Android devices
Community Q&A forum providing peer-to-peer financial advice and expertise
Financial education resources and calculators available to members
cons: Only 1 branch location and 0 ATMs limits accessibility and convenience
Documented service gap with unresponsive email communication despite repeated member requests
Modest asset base of $6.6 million reflects limited financial resources and services
Small member base of 600 members may result in limited negotiating power for rates and terms
Lobby currently closed, restricting in-person service availability
best_for: Healthcare workers and employees of member healthcare organizations seeking specialized banking
Members prioritizing personal service relationships over branch convenience
Individuals seeking community-based credit union membership with healthcare sector alignment
Savers interested in certificates of deposit and retirement account options
diagnosis: Valley Pride Federal Credit Union is best suited for healthcare sector employees within its membership eligibility criteria who value community banking relationships and are located near Kingston, Pennsylvania. The primary caveat is its severe infrastructure limitation—one branch with zero ATMs and a currently closed lobby—combined with documented digital communication gaps that may frustrate members seeking modern banking convenience.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
